findings: Hook directly stages the misconception with two answers from the same expression so a student sees why the rule matters before the rule arrives.
PEMDAS trap slide targets the most common left-to-right mistake (5 + 3 × 4 = 32 vs 17).
Same-level slide explicitly disarms the second trap students hit: treating M as ranked above D.
Both pause problems are answerable from the lesson and have visible step-by-step answer reveals.
Final path slide ends with a concrete Learn Portal action tied to the published assignment.
watch_points: Slide 09 (same-level rule) is denser than the others; keep narration pace slow there during final review.
